steps.name: Wire resolve_concern handler in server.ts with real concern-matcher and format-response
steps.description: Replace the NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub in src/shell/server.ts with the full resolve_concern handler: validate concern, probe each repo, read all nWave files via DocTreeReader, call matchConcernInSnapshot, aggregate results, format and return. Imports validateConcern, matchConcernInSnapshot from concern-matcher.ts and formatInvalidConcern, formatConcernNotFound, formatResolveConcernResponse from format-response.ts. Covers all 1 scenario in walking-skeleton.feature. scenario_name is the entry-point scenario; crafter enables it in RED_ACCEPTANCE.
criteria: resolve_concern tool is registered and callable over MCP stdio transport
Given a fixture repo with a feature-level decision file and an ADR both mentioning 'auth', resolve_concern('auth') returns at least one match
Each match includes a repo_name field identifying its source repo
retrieved_at is 'live (no cache)' in every response
steps.scenario_name: Agent resolves a concern and receives authoritative source with live timestamp

phases.name: Release 1: single-repo keyword matching and input validation

steps.name: Implement validateConcern in concern-matcher.ts
steps.description: Implement the validateConcern pure function: returns { valid: false, reason } when concern is empty after trimming or contains no alphanumeric character (/[a-zA-Z0-9]/ test); returns { valid: true } otherwise. No side effects, no IO. Covers scenarios 6-7 in release-1-single-repo-matching.feature (INVALID_CONCERN for empty string and punctuation-only input). scenario_name is the entry-point; crafter enables remaining INVALID_CONCERN scenario in the same TDD cycle.
criteria: resolve_concern('') returns error INVALID_CONCERN
resolve_concern('???') returns error INVALID_CONCERN
Error message explains concern must contain at least one letter or number
retrieved_at is present on INVALID_CONCERN responses
steps.scenario_name: Agent receives invalid input error for an empty concern

steps.name: Implement matchConcernInSnapshot and extend format-response.ts
steps.description: Implement matchConcernInSnapshot: case-insensitive substring matching across featureFiles (relevance: feature-level, including directory-name match), adrFiles (relevance: architecture-level), and claudeMdFile (relevance: repo-conventions). Snippet size-capped at 8000 chars with heading-alignment. Ordering: feature-level first, then architecture-level, then repo-conventions. Also add ConcernMatch, RejectedPath, ResolveConcernResponse, InvalidConcernError, ConcernNotFoundError types to format-response.ts and implement formatInvalidConcern, formatConcernNotFound, formatResolveConcernResponse formatters. Covers scenarios 1-5 in release-1-single-repo-matching.feature (feature-level match, ADR match, repo-conventions match, relevance ordering, CONCERN_NOT_FOUND). scenario_name is the entry-point; crafter enables remaining 4 scenarios one-at-a-time in the same TDD cycle.
criteria: resolve_concern('auth') with a feature-level file containing 'auth' returns a match with relevance feature-level and source file under auth-flow
resolve_concern('persistence') with only an ADR containing 'persistence' returns a match with relevance architecture-level and source file under architecture decisions directory
resolve_concern('testing') with only a repo conventions file containing 'testing' returns a match with relevance repo-conventions
When both a feature-level file and an ADR contain 'caching', the feature-level match appears first in the response
resolve_concern('graphql-federation') with no matching files returns error CONCERN_NOT_FOUND identifying the concern and listing searched repos
retrieved_at is 'live (no cache)' on all responses
steps.scenario_name: Agent finds a concern matched in a feature-level decision file

phases.name: Release 2: cross-repo scan and partial-failure resilience

steps.name: Implement cross-repo aggregation and probe-failure skip logic in server.ts handler
steps.description: Extend the resolve_concern handler to iterate all configured repos in config order, probe each before reading, skip repos whose probe fails (adding a skip warning), accumulate matches and rejectedPaths across all repos, and include skip warnings in the final response. CONCERN_NOT_FOUND when matches is empty, listing all successfully scanned repos and all skip warnings. Covers all 4 scenarios in release-2-cross-repo.feature (multi-repo match, single-repo match, partial probe failure, all-repos-unreachable). scenario_name is the entry-point; crafter enables remaining 3 scenarios one-at-a-time.
criteria: resolve_concern('data persistence') with two repos each containing 'data persistence' returns matches from both repos, each correctly identifying its source repo, with feature-level matches before architecture-level matches
resolve_concern('logging') with only repo-alpha containing 'logging' returns matches only from repo-alpha and shows both repos were searched
When repo-gamma has a non-existent doc path, resolve_concern('observability') still returns matches from repo-alpha and includes a notice that repo-gamma was skipped, and is not an error
When all configured repos have unreachable doc paths, resolve_concern('auth') returns CONCERN_NOT_FOUND with a notice per skipped repo and an empty searched_repos list
steps.scenario_name: Agent finds a concern present in multiple repos

phases.name: Release 3: rejected paths detection and partial-structure warning

steps.name: Implement detectRejectedPaths in concern-matcher.ts
steps.description: Implement detectRejectedPaths pure function: splits file content into paragraphs (blocks of non-blank lines separated by one or more blank lines), returns a RejectedPath for each paragraph that contains the concern (case-insensitive) AND at least one rejection keyword (as specified in ADR-005/D-CBQ-D2). Snippet capped at 1500 chars. matchConcernInSnapshot calls detectRejectedPaths on every matched file so rejected paths appear alongside matches. Also implements partial-structure warning in formatResolveConcernResponse (no feature-level matches → warning added). Covers all 5 scenarios in release-3-rejected-paths.feature. scenario_name is the entry-point; crafter enables remaining 4 scenarios one-at-a-time.
criteria: resolve_concern('auth') with an ADR containing a Rejected section mentioning OAuth2 returns at least one rejected path with type rejected_alternative, source file pointing to the ADR, and snippet containing OAuth2

resolve_concern('caching') with a feature-level file containing 'Out of scope: caching/invalidation layer' returns at least one rejected path with snippet containing 'caching' and type rejected_alternative

resolve_concern('auth') with an ADR that both accepts 'auth' and rejects an alternative returns the same source file in both matches and rejected_paths, the match carrying the full-file snippet and the rejected path carrying only the rejection paragraph
resolve_concern('logging') with no rejection language in any file returns a response where rejected_paths is present and contains no entries

resolve_concern('rate-limiting') with only an ADR mentioning 'rate-limiting' and no feature-level files returns a match with relevance architecture-level and a warning about the absence of feature-level decisions, and is not an error
steps.scenario_name: Rejected alternative surfaced from an architecture decision record
